name: FS Charles de Gaulle (R91)
detail: France's only aircraft carrier and the only nuclear-powered carrier outside the United States Navy. Commissioned in 2001, displaces 42,500 tons, carries up to 40 Rafale M and E-2C Hawkeye aircraft. It is the flagship of the French Navy and has deployed to the Gulf, the Indian Ocean, and the Mediterranean in multiple operations.

name: Dassault Rafale (F3-R/F4)
detail: France's principal multirole combat aircraft, produced entirely in France by Dassault Aviation. Capable of nuclear strike (ASMP-A missile), air-to-air, ground attack, reconnaissance, and maritime strike roles. France operates approximately 185 Rafale aircraft across the Armée de l'Air et de l'Espace and Aéronavale. The Rafale F4 standard (2024) adds enhanced networking and extended range capabilities.

name: Le Triomphant-class SSBNs (Force Océanique Stratégique)
detail: France's sea-based nuclear deterrent consists of four ballistic missile submarines (SSBN): Le Triomphant, Le Téméraire, Le Vigilant, and Le Terrible. At least one submarine is permanently on patrol. Each carries 16 M51 SLBMs with TN 75 thermonuclear warheads (range: 8,000+ km). The SNLE-3G successor program is under development for the 2030s.

name: Légion étrangère (French Foreign Legion)
detail: Elite military formation founded in 1831 that accepts recruits of any nationality. Approximately 8,000 legionnaires in 11 regiments are deployed worldwide, with bases in France, French Guiana, Djibouti, Mayotte, and UAE. The Legion has participated in virtually every French military operation since its foundation, including Mali, Afghanistan, and the Balkans.

name: GIGN (Groupe d'intervention de la Gendarmerie nationale)
detail: France's premier counterterrorism and hostage rescue unit, founded in 1974 after the Munich Olympics massacre. GIGN operators freed 229 hostages aboard Air France Flight 8969 (Algiers, 1994) and have conducted hundreds of operations worldwide. It serves under the Gendarmerie nationale and has approximately 400 personnel.

name: CAESAR Self-Propelled Howitzer
detail: French-designed 155mm wheeled self-propelled artillery system developed by Nexter Systems. France supplied 18 CAESAR systems to Ukraine in 2022 (later expanded), where they performed exceptionally. France operates 77 CAESAR systems in its army and has been an active deployer of these systems in Mali (Operation Barkhane) and other operations.

name: DGSE (Direction générale de la sécurité extérieure)
detail: France's foreign intelligence and counterespionage service, equivalent to the British MI6 or American CIA. DGSE operates globally with an estimated 7,000 personnel, conducting human intelligence, electronic surveillance, and covert action. It was central to France's Africa operations and reports directly to the Prime Minister and President.

name: Force de Frappe (French Nuclear Deterrent)
detail: France's independent nuclear force, comprising submarine-based (SNLE, ~80% of warheads) and air-launched (ASMP-A carried by Rafale aircraft) components. France has approximately 290 nuclear warheads and has maintained the world's third-largest nuclear arsenal since testing its first device in 1960. The force is independent of NATO's nuclear sharing arrangements.
